target.name: H2Pro
target.founder: Gideon Grader, Avner Rothschild, Hen Dotan, Talmon Marco

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

data.narrative:
H2Pro is a Series B-stage climate and energy company that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies—Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita—alongside one public comparable, alongside stage-progression methodology.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $190M. Peer-median analysis across the five identified comparables yields a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI). The peer cohort exhibits meaningful dispersion, reflecting variation in commercialization progress and technology differentiation within the sector.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $162M to $356M, with a median of $250M. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around H2Pro's relative positioning within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and market traction to date—factors that would justify placement toward either the lower (stage-progression) or upper (peer-median) end of the range. Data on H2Pro's revenue, burn rate, or product-market fit would materially tighten this range.
